author: Austin Crawford
title: Community Tree Lighting on the lawn of the Harbor Church
summary: Come one, come all to the Community Tree Lighting!
content:
Come one, come all to the Community Tree Lighting!
The Block Island Tourism Council invites you to help light the tree on the church lawn.... a tradition for over 15 years!
Tuesday, November 29th, on the lawn of the Harbor Church. Refreshments will be served, BI School students will be singing your favorite carols and Santa will be there to help us turn on the lights!
The fun starts at about 5:30 and we will light the tree as close to 6 as possible. (Sometimes we are a few minutes early....don’t miss it!)

author: Robert Lucier
title: Blues on the Beach
summary: The final Blues on the Beach for 2021 will be Wednesday, August 25th  from 5:30-7 pm.
content:
The final Blues on the Beach for 2021 will be Wednesday, August 25th  from 5:30-7 pm.

This will be a special reggae on the beach night. Featuring Root Steady from Providence/Block Island, RI. Come down and dance for the last event of the summer!! 

Thank you for all your support this year! This event is sponsored by:

BIRA (Block Island Residents Association)
BI Tourism Council
The Oar
Interstate navigation

Brought to you by the Block Island Chamber of Commerce — at Fred Benson Town Beach.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Shad Bloom 10K
summary: This spring race (an island favorite) takes runners over a scenic 10K course with breathtaking views of beautiful Block Island. The race includes a little bit of everything - dirt roads with stone walls and rolling pastures, a winding track overlooking the Atlantic, and at mile 4 racers meet "the wall" a steep, double tiered, narrow climb. Every mile is different. This race has a very loyal following. The race begins at 1:00 pm at the Block Island School. Questions? Please call 401-466-3223 ask for Dave or Mac. Or send us an email.
content:
This spring race (an island favorite) takes runners over a scenic 10K course with breathtaking views of beautiful Block Island. The race includes a little bit of everything - dirt roads with stone walls and rolling pastures, a winding track overlooking the Atlantic, and at mile 4 racers meet "the wall" a steep, double tiered, narrow climb. Every mile is different. This race has a very loyal following. The race begins at 1:00 pm at the Block Island School. Questions? Please call 401-466-3223 ask for Dave or Mac. Or send us an email. 

REGISTER HERE

author: Austin Crawford
title: 2026 Storm Trysail Club Block Island Race
summary: The historic Block Island Race, hosted by the Storm Trysail Club, returns on Friday, May 22, 2026, ushering in another season of premier offshore competition for sailors from across the Northeast and beyond. First organized in 1946 by returning war veterans seeking camaraderie and a return to normalcy, the Block Island Race has grown into a beloved ocean-racing classic—challenging crews with a scenic yet demanding course around Block Island and through the ever-changing waters of Long Island and Block Island Sounds.d through the waters of the Long Island and Block Island Sounds.
content:
The historic Block Island Race, hosted by the Storm Trysail Club, returns on Friday, May 22, 2026, ushering in another season of premier offshore competition for sailors from across the Northeast and beyond. First organized in 1946 by returning war veterans seeking camaraderie and a return to normalcy, the Block Island Race has grown into a beloved ocean-racing classic—challenging crews with a scenic yet demanding course around Block Island and through the ever-changing waters of Long Island and Block Island Sounds.d through the waters of the Long Island and Block Island Sounds.

Set against the stunning backdrop of Rhode Island’s coastal waters, the 2026 Block Island Race is expected to draw a competitive fleet of monohulls competing for top honors and trophies in their divisions. The race traditionally departs off Stamford, Connecticut, before rounding Block Island and returning, testing crews with a combination of strategic navigation, weather variability, and pure seamanship.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Film Festival
summary: The 2026 Block Island Film Festival is dedicated to the “Sundance Kid” — legendary filmmaker Robert Redford.
content:
The 2026 Block Island Film Festival is dedicated to the “Sundance Kid” — legendary filmmaker Robert Redford.

This year’s film festival will be held from May 26 - 28 at the Block Island Maritime Institute and Champlin’s Marina & Resort in New Harbor on Block Island.

The Block Island Film Festival boasts notable actors, directors, screenwriters, filmmakers, student filmmakers and entertainment professionals, on the beautiful resort destination known as Block Island, located 13 miles off the coast of Rhode Island. A consortium of global industry professionals gather to mingle with contemporaries, and the public, in intimate settings, such as film screenings, spotlight programs, tribute and award ceremonies, question and answer forums, and nightly social mixers.

This year’s film festival will operate all programming at the Block Island Maritime Institute and Champlin’s Marina & Resort in New Harbor, which is located at the edge of the Great Salt Pond. Tickets will be available at the door only. Please arrive at least 15 minutes prior to the beginning of each screening to purchase tickets. A full schedule of the festival’s programming is available at the Schedule tab.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Arts & Craft Guild at Block Island Historical Society
summary: There’s something about an island that attracts artists and craftspeople, and Block Island is no different. Maybe it’s the quality of the light and maybe it’s the more laid-back lifestyle, but the charms of the island find expression in the works created here.
content:
There’s something about an island that attracts artists and craftspeople, and Block Island is no different. Maybe it’s the quality of the light and maybe it’s the more laid-back lifestyle, but the charms of the island find expression in the works created here. 

If you’d like to take home an artistic reminder of beautiful Block Island, one of the best spots to browse and buy takes place outside of the Block Island Historical Society. Several times each summer, from 9 am to 1 pm, the lawn becomes a showplace and marketplace for members of the Block Island Arts and Crafts Guild. Choose among paintings, photographs, sculpture, jewelry, fabric arts, candles, and more. All of the items are handmade by local artists and would make a perfect keepsake for yourself or a gift for the folks back home.

This year, the dates are set for June 28, July 12,  July 26,  August 9,  August 23, September 13, and October 11. There’s no charge for admission to the Fair.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island 4th of July Fireworks
summary: The annual fireworks display will be at dusk on Tuesday, July 1st.  The more the merrier for this patriotic event, so bring the whole gang and all the accouterments — chairs, blankets, picnics, and glow sticks for the kids.
content:
The annual fireworks display will be at dusk on Tuesday, July 1st.  The more the merrier for this patriotic event, so bring the whole gang and all the accouterments — chairs, blankets, picnics, and glow sticks for the kids.

The show starts at dusk on a barge 2000 feet off Fred Benson Town Beach. Seen from any location, set up on the beach or in your backyard with friends and family.

 Ocean State Pyrotechnics will be putting on the fireworks display! They will be shooting them off the display from a barge off-shore! This will be very exciting!

If viewing from the water, please be sure to keep clear 1000 feet away from the barge for safety reasons.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Tri-State Canyon Shootout - Fishing Tournament at Champlin's
summary: JB Tackle hosts this Annual fishing event with competitions for a great variety of species: Albacore, Bigeye Tuna, Blue Marlin, Bluefin Tuna, Dolphin, Flounder, Sailfish, Sea Bass, Spearfish, Swordfish, Wahoo, White Marlin, Yellowfin Tuna. This year the tournament will be based out of Champlin's. So come down and check out the boats and the fish. The afternoon weigh-ins are not to be missed!
content: JB Tackle hosts this Annual fishing event with competitions for a great variety of species: Albacore, Bigeye Tuna, Blue Marlin, Bluefin Tuna, Dolphin, Flounder, Sailfish, Sea Bass, Spearfish, Swordfish, Wahoo, White Marlin, Yellowfin Tuna. This year the tournament will be based out of Champlin's. So come down and check out the boats and the fish. The afternoon weigh-ins are not to be missed!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Great Salt Pond Swim 11
summary: The Committee for the Great Salt Pond’s 11th annual swim, known as The Great Salt Pond Swim 11–2026.
content:
The Committee for the Great Salt Pond’s 11th annual swim, known as The Great Salt Pond Swim 11–2026.

This will be a one (1) mile swim limited to 150 individuals, starting at Andy’s Way Beach, swimming out toward Beane Point, and returning to Andy’s Way Beach.

The swimmer registration fee for the event is $60.00. Registration is now open. This is a great opportunity for first-time open water swimmers. The swim course is in a protected area with not much chop. In addition, the swim will take place along a straight line, marked by buoys.

This swim will be an annual fundraiser, serving to support our efforts to protect The Great Salt Pond, and provide funds for additional water testing in the recreational swim areas. 

Swim registration will close July 26, 2026 at 1:00 pm.
There will be on-site registration in 2026. 

Click the link to register https://rb.gy/g0asj5
address: Andy's Way New Shoreham Washington County

title: 2026 Block Island Triathalon
summary: ¼ mile swim, 12 mile bike, 4 mile run
content:
• ¼ mile swim, 12 mile bike, 4 mile run

The race will begin and end at the Fred Benson Town Beach. The swim is held in front of the Beach Pavilion. The bike route consists of biking around the West Side twice. The run is held on the East Beaches. Questions? Please call 401-466-3223 ask for Dave or Mac. Or send us a email recreation@new-shoreham.com

Click the link to register https://rebrand.ly/lryqd0j

author: Austin Crawford
title: Mary D Ball at Sullivan House
summary: The "Event of the Season" on Block Island.
content:
The "Event of the Season" on Block Island.
The evening begins with live music and dinner and ends with the crowd dancing into the night to the DJ.

This fundraiser supports the Mary D. Fund. The Mary D. Fund is named for Mary Donnelly - it uses the funds to assist families and individuals with bills they cannot manage in the winter months. Many senior citizens rely on the assistance with heating bills, medical bills and electric bills.
address: Corn Neck Rd New Shoreham Washington County
